examples.english: Do you have any friends other than these?
All the travellers arrived but one.

senses.gloss: no, not, un-, non-, in-, dis-
someone or something else; someone or something other than; other; others

examples.text: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ
examples.english: Except for those who believe and work good deeds: their reward will not wear off.

examples.text: إِنَّ اللَّٰهَ لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا مَا بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ

senses.gloss: masculine plural of غَيُور (ḡayūr, “zealous, very jealous, very enthusiastic”)

etymology.text:
Compare غَارَ (ḡāra, “to be jealous, to display zeal”).

See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

From English gear.
